evidences.title: Every confirmed LPL roster for 2026 - Esports Insider
evidences.content: Image Credit: Anyone’s Legend Weibo Starting off this list is Anyone’s Legend, the most consistent LPL team during the 2025 season. After a strong season, which saw Anyone’s Legend finish in the top eight at Worlds 2025, the organisation was able to retain all five members for yet another season. That said, the LPL 2025 Split 2 champion had to bring in a new head coach following Wong ‘Tabe’ Pak Kan’s departure. The new head coach is Kwon ‘Helper’ Yeong-jae, formerly of Gen.G, and he will be supported by Jiang ‘Teacherma’ Chen. #### Anyone’s Legend 2026 LPL Roster Top: Flandre Jungle: Tarzan Mid: Shanks ADC: Hope Support: Kael ## BiliBili Gaming (BLG) BiliBili Gaming (BLG) brings back Xun as starting jungler for 2026 Image Credit: BiliBili Gaming [...] Invictus Gaming unveils major roster shakeup for 2026 Image Credit: Invictus Gaming As the most popular team in the LPL, many fans were wondering whether Invictus Gaming would run its 2025 roster for another year. Unfortunately, that isn’t going to happen, as ADC Chen ‘GALA’ Wei left and top laner Kang ‘TheShy’ Seung-lok has decided to take a temporary break from pro play. However, the team was able to extend the contracts for jung...

evidences.title: Every Team's New Roster in LPL 2026 - YouTube
evidences.content: will start his place, reuniting with long-term LDL teammate Nao. The 2026 Top Esports lineup is 369, Nao, Cream, Jachi, and Fenguin. They've also brought in Poppy as head coach from Edward Gaming. This is Top Esports betting on homegrown talent and promoting from within their own roster and organization while their Golden Boy recovers. JDG underwent a complete transformation. They are keeping exactly one player from their 2025 roster, top laner Shiau. Everyone else is gone. Shun went to Billy Billy Gaming. Scout returns as a prodigal son from the LPL to the LCK where he's joining Nongshin Redforce. Hayes went to T1 replacing long-term AD carry Gumayusi and J joined ninjas in pajamas. So, who's coming in? JDG made a massive bet on the LCP, turning their eyes to incredibly dominant mid [...] who's returning after sitting out in split 3 of 2025. His last team before his break was JDG, where he was a major part of their success in years prior, but struggled pretty heavily to find his footing in 2025. The roster features Shear in top, Crocco in jungle, Bulldog in mid, and Monxen or Ishin depending on your pronunciation at ADC, and Missing making his return to pro play in support. Rou...

evidences.title: 2025 World Championship - Play-In
evidences.content: ## Format [edit] Play-In - October 14th + The 4th seed of the regions that obtained an additional slot from MSI compete in a single elimination match. + Winner qualifies into Swiss Stage. + The match is Bo5 using Fearless Draft. ## Play-In [edit] October 14, 2025 - 16:15 CST T1 3:1(Bo5) Invictus Gaming MVP: Oner | Bans | | Game 1 | | Game 2 | | Game 3 | | Game 4 | Casters: Australia Atlus, United States Kobe Invictus Gaming won the coin toss and chose blue side for Game 1. evidences.title: LPL 2025 Power Rankings/Predictions : r/leagueoflegends - Reddit
evidences.content: 9. RNG Xiaoxu, Milkyway, Tangyuan, JiaQi, Lele/Niket I know, I know, terrible timing. Coming off an 0-3 performance at Demacia Cup including a humiliating defeat to IG, it's easy to say RNG will just be terrible next split. However, I refuse to get carried away by bad performances at Demacia Cup (only positive vibes around Christmas, right?) and RNG have the 2 best players among all the non-playoff teams in Xiaoxu and Milkyway. I put a lot of faith in them also finalizing the Niket signing, which gives them a botlane with existing chemistry and at least one half that has proven to be LPL-capable. I'm also a fan of their coaching additions, especially Comet who I have fond memories of from 2021 Nongshim. Look out for: Xiaoxu, Milkyway 10. FPX sheer, sorrow, Care, bat, Jwei [...] 11. LGD Gaming sasi, Climber, xqw, Shaoye, Ycx LGD reacted to losing their strong point in haichao/Meteor by replacing them with their LDL winning prospect duo, which is honestly as good as you can ask for from a team that works on a budget as tight as LGD do. Meteor is staying on as a coach which should greatly aid in Climber's development, as Meteor is one of the smartest pathing junglers of recent year...

evidences.title: T1 defeats KT Rolster to win League of Legends Worlds 2025
evidences.content: # T1 defeats KT Rolster to win League of Legends Worlds 2025 Lea Maas Lea Maas News Writer x-icon linkedin-icon Lea is an esports journalist with too many interests and too little time. Covering esports stories for more than 3 years, she likes to spend her days (and nights) watching and analysing competitive VALORANT. Lea is also deeply invested in DEI issues and promoting mental health awareness within her industry. Read Full Bio calendar-icon November 9, 2025 whatsapp-icon x-icon linkedin-icon T1 at LoL Worlds 2025 Image credit: Christina Oh/Riot Games T1 has secured its dream of the three-peat by winning today’s League of Legends World Championship 2025 Grand Final versus LCK rival KT Rolster 3-2. [...] T1’s Faker. Image credit: Colin Young-Wolff/Riot Games KT began its Worlds journey with an undefeated Group Stage performance. In the Knockout Stage, the team then toppled giants CTBC Flying Oyster (3-0) and Gen.G Esports (3-1) to earn a shot at Championship glory. However, to lift its very first Worlds trophy, KT had to defeat its long-standing rival T1, led by the inaugural Hall of Legends inductee and five-time world champion Faker. Despite its previous accomplishments, T1...

evidences.content: Casters: Drakos, Chronicler, Kobe KT Rolster won the coin toss and selected blue side for game 1. View match details ## Format [edit] Qualification + MSI champion qualifies into Worlds if they advance to their regional playoffs + The top three teams besides MSI champion from each region (15 total) participate in Swiss Stage + The 4th seed of MSI winning region (LCK) & runner-up region (LPL) start from Play-In Play-In - October 14th + The 4th seed of the regions that obtained an additional slot from MSI compete in a single elimination match. + Winner qualifies into Swiss Stage. + The match is Bo5 using Fearless Draft. evidences.title: Split 1: LPL Standings - LoL Esports
evidences.content: ## Split 1: LPL Standings Jan – Mar ##### Split 1 overview The 2025 League of Legends Esports season kicks off with each region running their own Split format and style. The top team from each region qualifies for First Stand. 1st Place ###### 1st Place Qualifies for First Stand ##### Event Phases Every team from this region participate. ###### Regular Season Teams are broken up into four groups. Each group plays single round robin matches. The top two teams from each qualfiy for Playoffs. ###### Playoffs The top eight teams come together for a double elimination bracket with the winner qualifying for First Stand. ##### Participating Teams All teams in the region participate in this event EDWARD GAMING EDG Invictus Gaming IG LGD GAMING LGD Oh My God OMG [...] EDG Invictus Gaming IG LGD GAMING LGD Oh My God OMG FunPlus Phoenix FPX Royal Never Give Up RNG Suzhou LNG Esports LNG Xi'an Team WE WE Beijing JDG Esports JDG WeiboGaming WBG TOP ESPORTS TES BILIBILI GAMING BLG Riot Games League of Legends © 2026 Riot Games.
